raw.ability details: * The javelin is automatically fired 1.1 seconds after reaching maximum charge.

raw.official description: Regenerate 100 health per second while charging Terra Surge.
raw.heal: 100 per second

heal.raw: 100 per second

raw.official description: Fortify briefly grants an additional 100% decaying overhealth when activated.
raw.ability details: * ''Removed in Season 18.'' * Bonus overhealth decays to the base value over 0.25 seconds when the duration ends. * Also applies to Fortify gained from using [[File:Ultimate_TerraSurge.png|sub|link=Orisa#Terra Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Terra Surge|Terra Surge]] but starts decaying after 1 second instead.

raw.official description: Critical hits from your primary fire refund heat.
raw.ability details:
* ''Removed in Season 18.''
* [[Critical hit]]s refund 100% of the heat generated by the shot.

raw.official description: While Fortify is active, Orisa has no movement speed reduction and no heat generation.
Gain temporary health, reducing all damage taken and becoming unstoppable.
raw.ability details: * Orisa is immune to [[critical hit]]s while active. ** This doesn't affect forced critical hit damage, such as [[Mauga]]'s [[File:Volatile_Chaingun_icon.png|middle|link=Mauga#Volatile Chaingun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Volatile Chaingun|Volatile Chaingun]] hitting the burning Orisa or [[Reinhardt]]'s [[File:Ability-reinhardt5.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Earthshatter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Earthshatter|Earthshatter]] * "Unstoppable" causes Orisa to become immune to crowd control [[status effect]]s, including slow, root, knockback, stun, knocked down, gravity and [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack's]] ability lockout. ** Orisa can still be affected by non-cc status effects, including [[damage over time]], [[anti-heal]] and Hack's marking for reveal and [[File:Ability Sombra Opportunist.png|sub|link=Sombra#Opportunist|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Opportunist|Opportunist]] bonus damage. ** Walking through [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]] with Fortify active or activating it after being caught destroys it. ** Orisa takes no damage from [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] or [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]]. * Orisa can be lifted by Lifeweaver's [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] and pulled by [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] while Fortified. * All temporary health is immediately lost when the effect ends. * Cooldown starts when the ability is activated. * Can be manually canceled by reactivating the ability.

raw.official description: Orisa temporarily reduces damage she takes and cannot be affected by movement-impairing effects.
raw.ability details: * Reworked in ''Overwatch 2''. * Orisa takes no [[headshot]] damage. * Orisa becomes immune to all movement-related [[Crowd Control]] effects, including stun, sleep, freeze, pin, knock down, knock back, pull, slow, root, etc. ** Orisa can escape [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]], [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], and [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]]. ** Orisa takes no damage from [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] or [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]]. * Orisa can be [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|hacked]] by [[Sombra]] while using Fortify, but this will not interrupt the ability. * Allies behind Orisa are not protected from abilities such as [[File:Ability-reinhardt5.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Earthshatter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Earthshatter|Earthshatter]] or [[File:Ability-Junkrat5.png|sub|link=Junkrat#RIP-Tire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#RIP-Tire|RIP-Tire]]. * [[Armor]] is applied after damage reduction. Attacks consisting of 16.667... or lower damage (e.g. Sombra's [[File:Ability Sombra Machine Pistol.png|middle|link=Sombra#Machine Pistol|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Machine Pistol|Machine Pistol]]) are reduced by 70%. Beams are reduced by 58%.

abilities.name: Javelin Spin
shot_type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
raw.official description: Spin your javelin to destroy projectiles and block melee attacks, while also pushing enemies and increasing forward speed.
raw.damage:
20 (first hit)
5.3 (subsequent hits)
raw.cast time: 0 + 0.1 seconds
raw.ability keywords: negate projectile::ignore barrier;;negate effect::ignore melee::channeled::speed cap
raw.ability details: * Deals up to 100 damage over the duration. * Orisa cannot shoot or use quick melee during Javelin Spin. * Using [[File:Ability_EnergyJavelin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Energy Javelin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Energy Javelin|Energy Javelin]] interrupts Javelin Spin. * [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] can be used simultaneously with Javelin Spin without interrupting it. * Javelin Spin is a [[channeled]] ability; the ability ends immediately if Orisa becomes [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], putting the ability on cooldown. * Orisa gains a [[movement speed]] bonus while moving forward or diagonally forward while the spin animation is active and a smaller bonus for 2 seconds after it ends. * Cannot block beam, AoE or movement-based attacks, such as [[File:Ability-genji3.png|sub|link=Genji#Swift Strike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Swift Strike|Swift Strike]] or abilities marked as Strong Projectile, such as [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|sub|link=Sigma#Accretion|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Accretion|Accretion]] and [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]]. ** Abilities that create an AoE or leave a deployable, but travel as a projectile can be blocked before they land. ** Cannot block [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]] despite it being marked as a Melee ability, due to its stun interrupting the channel first. * Damage and knockback can be line of sight blocked by [[barrier]]s. (Damaging "through" barriers, like Reinhardt's shield, happens when the hitbox's origin is pushed past it.) Negate Projectile property fully pierces barriers. * Cooldown starts when the spin animation ends. * Can be manually canceled by reactivating the ability. * The ability is incorrectly marked with Movement keyword, it is not interrupted or disabled by [[Hinder]]. * Negate projectile mentions the ability "reflects" projectiles, however it only absorbs them.

raw.shot type: [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile
raw.official description: Javelin Spin is replaced with a barrier.
raw.radius:
7.4 meter width
3.8 meter height
2.9 meter depth
raw.ability details: * Cooldown begins immediately upon activation. * The projectile bounces off of walls and terrain-like objects and deploys the barrier upon landing on ground. ** The barrier cannot be deployed on objects with a walkable surface, such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. The projective will bounce off of them like walls. * The barrier extends slightly below where it is deployed, for example if it is on a ledge or a payload. * The projectile takes approximately 5.5 seconds to land if fired directly upwards. * Prevents Orisa from firing her Fusion Driver or using Energy Javelin for 0.5 seconds

raw.shot type: [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile
raw.official description: Orisa throws out a stationary barrier that can protect her and her allies from enemy fire.
raw.ability details: * Replaced by [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]] in ''Overwatch 2''. * Cooldown begins immediately. * The projectile ignores [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], and [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]]. * The [[barrier]] is shaped like a football net. * The barrier extends slightly below where it is deployed in the case that it is on a ledge or a payload. * The barrier can be deployed without interrupting [[File:Ability Orisa Fusion Driver.png|middle|link=Orisa#Fusion Driver|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fusion Driver|Fusion Driver]]'s reload.

abilities.name: Augmented Fusion Driver

raw.fire rate: 10 rounds/s
raw.reload time:
3 seconds (cooldown after overheat)
1.9 seconds (recharge from full heat)
raw.dps: 140 while firing (99.42 overall w/ recharge, 86.84 w/ overheat)
raw.ability details:
* Damage per second: 140 while firing (100.88 overall w/ recharge, 86.84 w/ overheat)
* Max spread is reached after 21 shots.
* Uses a heat-based mechanic instead of ammo/reload.
** Fusion Driver generates 2.04% heat per projectile fired.
*** This amounts to 50 projectiles or 5 seconds of continuous fire before overheating.
** While not firing, Fusion Driver instantly starts cooling, recharging 53% heat per second. It takes 1.9 seconds to restore 100% of the heat meter.
** Once overheated, the weapon cannot be used for 3 seconds.
** Abilities can be used without interrupting the recharge even while the weapon is overheated.

3 seconds (cooldown after overheat)
1.9 seconds (recharge from full heat)

abilities.name: Energy Javelin

raw.shot type: [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile
raw.official description: Launch your javelin at an enemy to stun and knock them back. More effective if the enemy hits a wall.
raw.damage:
70 (direct impact)
40 (wall collision)
raw.cast time: 0.3 + 0.4 seconds
raw.ability details: * Hits the first enemy in its path, delivering a 0.45 seconds micro-stun. Deals extra damage and stuns again for 0.4 seconds if they are pushed into a wall or a terrain-like object, such as an [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. * Has a slider angle of 45°, meaning if the target enemy hits a wall at an angle greater than 45° from the surface normal, they will not receive the effects of a wall impact. * If the javelin hits D.Va's [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]] while it is moving, the Mech will be stunned and will stop moving.

raw.official description: Launch a graviton charge which can be detonated with the same action. The sphere slows and pulls nearby enemies to it.
raw.ability details: * Replaced by [[File:Ability_EnergyJavelin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Energy Javelin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Energy Javelin|Energy Javelin]] in ''Overwatch 2''. * Automatically activates upon touching a surface. * Can be manually activated by pressing [[File:Key mouse 2.png|20x20px|link=]] key again. * Enemies in range are pulled to the center and are briefly slowed until 0.65 seconds after activation. * The projectile itself goes through [[barrier]]s, but the pull effect is blocked by them.

abilities.name: Supercharger

raw.official description: Orisa deploys a device to increase damage inflicted by allies within her line of sight.
raw.ability details: * Replaced by [[File:Ultimate_TerraSurge.png|sub|link=Orisa#Terra Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Terra Surge|Terra Surge]] in ''Overwatch 2''. *Orisa passively generates 1% Ult Charge every 3.36 seconds. *Enemy [[barrier]]s break line of sight. *The effect lingers for 1 second if line of sight is broken or if a player is out of range. *Can be [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|hacked]] by [[Sombra]], which disables it for 10 seconds. *The Supercharger projectile experiences 3x normal gravity (60 meters per second squared)

abilities.name: Terra Surge
shot_type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
raw.official description: Sweep in enemies and anchor down, gaining the effects of Fortify and charging up a surge of damage. Use Primary Fire to unleash the surge early.
raw.damage:
18.8/s (periodic damage)
72 - 500 (release)
raw.cast time:
None (initial cast)
0.2 seconds (release)
0.3 seconds (recovery)
raw.ability details: * When activated, Orisa starts generating charge and enemies are pulled toward a point above Orisa and take periodic damage while in the area. ** Charge starts accumulating and Fortify is gained immediately upon button press, but the pull and periodic damage start taking effect after a 0.4 second delay. ** The pull will not stop until the target reaches the point or leaves the area. ** Orisa cannot move, shoot or use any other abilities until the cast is finished. * When released, enemies in the area take damage equal to the charge percentage and are [[knocked back]] depending on the charge. ** Charge starts at 25% and accumulates at a rate of 62.5% per second in the first 2.5 seconds. ** Charge accumulates at a rate of 212.5/s in the last 1.5 seconds. ** Charge can be released after 0.75 seconds (~72%) and will be automatically released upon reaching 500%. *** Including the periodic damage, the total damage is ~83 at minimum charge and 571 at maximum charge. ** The minimum time required to kill a 250hp enemy is ~2.7 seconds (around 210% charge). * Initial pull, periodic damage and final damage all pierce enemy [[barrier]]s, but are line of sight blocked by terrain and terrain-like [[objects]], such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. * Orisa can be lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] (either allied or enemy) during Terra Surge. * [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] cannot be used on Orisa while channeling Terra Surge. Activating Terra Surge will also interrupt an active pull. * Orisa does gains ultimate charge with environmental kill, but require her suicide moment first. ([[Ilios]]'s well is the best possible way to do so.)
